page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled as 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps managed by the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance, geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, the high core count compensates for this, yielding average multi-core performance across stress-ng multi-core, geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is a highlight, with small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 25%, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidth remains average. Cryptographic operations also perform strongly, as evidenced by the Passmark CPU encryption test ranking in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5ad.24xlarge offers high resource density for workloads that can parallelize tasks to overcome the lower single-core clock speed. The inclusion of 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local scratch space without the added cost of external block storage. This profile is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale data processing or redis caching layers, whereas it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or heavy large-model LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 20 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression are weak, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core performance and database operations achieve average ratings. Notably, small-block cached memory bandwidth and cryptographic encryption tests rank in the top 25%. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized workloads, making it suitable for multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, while remaining less suited for single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD, offering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance despite weaker single-core speeds.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose platform combining extensive multi-core processing, fast local NVMe storage, and strong memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores via AMD EPYC 7571 processors.
Equipped with 384 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Strong performance in cryptographic encryption and small-block cached memory bandwidth.
Average multi-core CPU performance contrasted by weak single-core execution speeds.
Delivers 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth under the Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for multi-threaded database operations, web serving, and parallel processing.
